perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information for the sole purpose of evaluating and negotiating the Business Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the Business Transaction, and potentially for related future business ventures between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase introduces ambiguity by suggesting uses beyond the initial Business Transaction, contrasting with the 'sole purpose' clause. This might allow the Receiving Party to use confidential information outside of the Business Transaction, conflicting with the original intent. In Section 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use Restriction . The Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the Business Transaction, and potentially for related future business ventures between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the use of confidential information to 'sole purpose of evaluating and negotiating the Business Transaction'. The changed text broadens this to include 'potentially for related future business ventures between the parties'. This contradicts the original intent of sole use and is a strong contradiction. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, indicating the contradiction is introduced within the same section of the document. This fits the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information to its employees, representatives, and consultants, regardless of whether their participation is directly related to the Business Transaction, provided they are informed of the confidential nature of the information.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the circle of individuals who can access Confidential Information without requiring them to be directly involved in the Business Transaction or formally bound by the Agreement's terms, conflicting with the original, more restrictive clause. Making the employees, representatives, and consultant have the right to access the content, conflicts in-text in Section 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Restrictions and Exceptions . The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information to its employees, representatives, and consultants, regardless of whether their participation is directly related to the Business Transaction, provided they are informed of the confidential nature of the information. The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Receiving Party is not prohibited from disclosing Confidential Information it is legally compelled to disclose (by deposition, subpoena, civil investigative demand, court order or otherwise), provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of any such required disclosure so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained and the Disclosing Party has not waived compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party is advised by opinion of counsel it is legally required to furnish and will exercise reasonable efforts to limit the scope and content of such disclosure and obtain confidential treatment of such Confidential Information, Furthermore, the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement do not apply with respect to any Confidential Information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than from the improper action of the Receiving Party, (b) is developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, (c) was in the Receiving Party’s possession before receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (d) is received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not subject to an obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited disclosure of Confidential Information to employees and representatives whose participation was necessary for the Business Transaction and who agreed to be bound by the agreement. The changed text broadens this to include consultants regardless of their involvement in the transaction, as long as they are informed of the confidential nature. The Contradicted Text is essentially the same as the changed text, and as the explanation states, this contradicts the original clause because it permits disclosure to a broader group without requiring them to be formally bound by the agreement's terms. This creates an in-text contradiction based on the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will endeavor to limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using a degree of care deemed appropriate by its internal policies.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care' with 'degree of care deemed appropriate by its internal policies,' this change introduces subjectivity and potentially lowers the standard of care. This may conflict with the Disclosing Party's expectation of a consistent, reasonable standard. Making the use and distribution of the data only subjected to internal policies, can conflict with the agreement as a whole if those policies does not align with the rules of the agreement. Section 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Restrictions and Exceptions . The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will endeavor to limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using a degree of care deemed appropriate by its internal policies. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Receiving Party is not prohibited from disclosing Confidential Information it is legally compelled to disclose (by deposition, subpoena, civil investigative demand, court order or otherwise), provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of any such required disclosure so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained and the Disclosing Party has not waived compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party is advised by opinion of counsel it is legally required to furnish and will exercise reasonable efforts to limit the scope and content of such disclosure and obtain confidential treatment of such Confidential Information, Furthermore, the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement do not apply with respect to any Confidential Information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than from the improper action of the Receiving Party, (b) is developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, (c) was in the Receiving Party’s possession before receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (d) is received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not subject to an obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 'reasonable degree of care' for protecting confidential information. The changed text replaces this with a 'degree of care deemed appropriate by its internal policies.' This introduces subjectivity and potentially lowers the standard of care, as the internal policies may not align with the Disclosing Party's expectation of a reasonable standard. This conflicts with the expectation implied in the Contradicted Text, specifically in the phrase 'who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement,' because it is now unclear what standard of care is required since the internal policies are now the main standard and they may conflict with the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement states the entire understanding of the parties regarding the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and it cannot be altered or amended without a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement states the general understanding of the parties regarding the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and it may be altered or amended through mutual agreement, including electronic communication deemed acceptable by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'entire understanding' to 'general understanding' and allowing amendments via 'electronic communication,' this change introduces uncertainty about what constitutes the complete agreement and how it can be modified. This contradicts the requirement for a signed writing, potentially leading to disputes over valid amendments. Section 8
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement and Amendment . This Agreement states the general understanding of the parties regarding the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and it may be altered or amended through mutual agreement, including electronic communication deemed acceptable by both parties. This Agreement will not be construed to create any obligation on the part of either party to retain the other party’s services or to compensate the other party in any manner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agreement from stating the 'entire understanding' to 'general understanding' and allows amendments via 'electronic communication,' directly contradicting the original intent for alterations to be in a signed writing. The contradicted text is nearly identical to the changed text. Therefore, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction as it now permits changes to the original agreement without a formal signed writing.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will promptly return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all documents or other materials furnished by the Disclosing Party constituting Confidential Information, together with all copies thereof in the possession, custody or control of the Receiving Party, without retaining any copies.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will make a reasonable effort to return or destroy documents or other materials furnished by the Disclosing Party constituting Confidential Information, but may retain copies for archival purposes subject to anonymization.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will promptly return or destroy' to 'will make a reasonable effort to return or destroy' and allowing retention of anonymized copies, this change weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. This contradicts the Disclosing Party's expectation that all copies will be returned or destroyed, as originally agreed, introducing ambiguity in Section 9
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will make a reasonable effort to return or destroy documents or other materials furnished by the Disclosing Party constituting Confidential Information, but may retain copies for archival purposes subject to anonymization. Any destruction must be confirmed by the Receiving Party to the Disclosing Party in writing. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth herein, the Receiving Party is not required to return or destroy computer files stored in the ordinary course of its business as a result of automated back-up procedures (subject, however, to the confidentiality restrictions set forth herein).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party 'will promptly return or destroy' confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'make a reasonable effort to return or destroy' and allows the retention of anonymized copies. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, indicating the change introduces ambiguity by allowing the receiving party to retain anonymized copies. The original requirement was for all copies to be returned or destroyed. Therefore, this change creates a contradiction, as the changed and contradicted text is not requiring to return/destroy ALL confidential information.
